# feat(nexus): subapp mounting (#447)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `140424aa6870` — feat(nexus): subapp mounting (#447)

**Body**:

Adds `Nexus.mount(path, subapp)` so multi-gateway FastAPI
compositions (e.g. `src/kailash/api/gateway.py`'s 693 LOC
WorkflowAPIGateway pattern) can migrate to Nexus without
collapsing their composition boundaries into a flat handler
registry.

The implementation delegates to Starlette's native Mount via
`FastAPI.mount()` — which already handles prefix stripping,
per-subapp middleware/routing, and recursive composition
correctly. Nexus contributes: validation (path shape, non-None
subapp, duplicate rejection), introspection (`MountInfo` records
every mount for audit), pre-gateway queueing (mounts called
before init are deferred until the gateway exists), and
Nexus-aware subapp resolution (a mounted `Nexus` delegates
to its `fastapi_app`, so the child's full middleware/handler
stack applies to mounted requests).

Tests cover the four load-bearing invariants: path prefix
stripping, subapp middleware propagation, recursive composition,
and dispatch correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
